customer_anxiety_profile: Low anxiety, high cooperation. Tony is a 75-year-old retiree who called back unprompted and engaged throughout. His primary stated motivation is cost — 'see what it costs.' He has mild tech limitations (self-disclosed) and a busy medical appointment schedule. His one procedural caution was around the credit pull (credit scores locked, needs 2-3 days to unlock) — he was reassured this was not a live pull, but the closer should be aware the credit check has not occurred.
unresolved_concerns: Credit scores are currently locked — Tony said he needs 2-3 days to unlock them. Closer should confirm credit status before or at the start of the appointment.

Tony is on Dominion Energy's budget billing program — his stated bill of $92.08 is a smoothed monthly average, not a consumption-based cost. The closer should pull actual kWh data (collected verbally during this call) to frame the savings comparison accurately rather than anchoring to the $92 figure.
Roof age was not captured. Tony has lived at the home 3 years and described it as a 'brand new home' — low risk, but not confirmed.
sensitive_topics_raised: Age and tech limitations — Tony self-disclosed as 75 and described technology as 'way over my head.' Closer should not assume digital fluency for the appointment (e.g., navigating a design presentation online).

Active medical schedule — Tony is managing multiple doctor and specialist appointments. The appointment was booked around this schedule. Closer should treat the time slot as firm and not request rescheduling casually.
exact_promises_rep_made: Appointment confirmed for Wednesday the 22nd at 9:30 AM.
Confirmation text will be sent with date and time of the meeting.
KWh data will be sent to the design team to build a design for Tony's home.
closer_should_know: Tony's $92.08 bill is a budget-billed average — his actual consumption cost may be higher. The kWh readback Randy collected is the accurate data for the design. Frame savings against actual consumption, not the smoothed bill figure, or Tony will correctly note the numbers don't match what he pays.
